title: Author cold-start deck (~50 cards) from one open/PD source (≥2 note types, ≥1 open-media card)
context: The first deck proves that the open-flashcards pipeline works end-to-end: from an open/PD source through card authoring, style-guide compliance, accuracy review, and deterministic build to an installable .apkg. It must exercise at least two note types and include at least one openly-licensed image card to validate the media pipeline. The subject must be low-risk to avoid the high-stakes expert-review gate at this stage.
objective: Author approximately 50 flashcards from one clearly open/PD source, covering a low-risk subject, using at least 2 note types and at least 1 openly-licensed image card, all conforming to the style guide v1 and data-001 schema.
acceptanceCriteria: All cards sourced from one clearly open/PD source; per-card source URL, SPDX license identifier, and attribution string recorded in the JSON.
No content from third-party Anki decks or copyrighted sources; no proprietary question bank content.
Low-risk subject chosen (not medical, legal, financial, safety, or high-stakes exam content).
Exercises at least 2 note types (e.g., Basic and Cloze) and includes at least 1 card with an openly-licensed image; image license and attribution recorded separately.
Every card conforms to the card-writing style guide v1: atomic, unambiguous, accessible HTML, no color-only cues, image alt-text present.
Every card has a stable, deterministic GUID per the data-001 GUID rules.
Every card scored on the 4-dimension accuracy rubric against the source; nothing below 3/4 on any dimension is included.
Output is valid CrowdAnki JSON in decks/; output license is compatible with the source.
output: A CrowdAnki JSON deck file in decks/ (~50 cards, ≥2 note types, ≥1 image card), with full per-card provenance/attribution, accuracy-reviewed and ready for the build tool.
